findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\]"` hits `weightedDegreeZeroClassHom` at line 66, `weightedDegreeZeroQuotientEquivPicZero` at line 125, `degreeZeroClassHom` at line 172, `degreeZeroQuotientEquivWeightedDegreeZeroOne` at line 246, and `degreeZeroQuotientEquivUnweightedPicZero` at line 271; lines 81-93, 135-153, 180-192, 257-267, and 280-302 already provide the public normal-form API.
findings.fix: Keep the characteristic lemmas public but restructure the definitions/proofs so these bodies are not exposed, or reduce the exposed surface to declarations whose computation is genuinely intended API.
findings.issue: The public homs and quotient equivalences are marked `@[expose]`, so downstream code can depend on the chosen transport/first-isomorphism implementation despite the supplied apply and mk lemmas.

findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\]" TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor.lean` hits the new equivalence at line 491; lines 510-520 give the intended public behavior by simp lemmas.
findings.fix: Hide the equivalence body and rely on the coercion/apply lemmas, exposing only if a downstream target actually needs definitional unfolding of this transport.
findings.issue: `degreeZeroSubgroupEquivWeightedDegreeZeroOne` is also exposed even though consumers are given coercion simp lemmas, making the concrete subtype-transport body public API.

summary: The quotient model has the expected characteristic lemmas, but it still makes implementation bodies part of the public API. The author’s module-opacity objection explains why the current proofs use unfolding, but it does not establish that downstream consumers must unfold these definitions.
